dialogs.text: It’s the final moments of your hackathon journey. You’ve learned what hackathons are, how teams work, and how ideas are built. Now, one last challenge remains — the pitch.
You check your laptop. On screen: Presentation slides, Script draft, GitHub repository, Website preview, Devpost submission form
It’s no longer just about presenting. It’s about documenting, Publishing and Submitting.
Okay… five-minute pitch video. Public repo. Live site. Project description. No pressure.
Today, your idea becomes visible to the world.

You check your checklist: Record 5-minute MP4 demo, Upload to YouTube (Public or Unlisted), Push final code to GitHub, Deploy live website, Complete project description, Submit all links before deadline.
I’ve never presented anything before. What if I freeze up?
Take a deep breath, smile, and keep going. Everyone’s first pitch feels scary — that’s also what makes it powerful.
So… how do I even start?
With a story. Always with a story.
Let me show you the anatomy of a great pitch.
There are five thing you need to know — INTRO, PROBLEM, SOLUTION, IMPACT, CLOSING.
These are your five building blocks. Master them, and you’ll pitch like a pro.
First — the INTRO. You grab attention. Start with a story, a question, or a powerful fact.
Next comes the Problem. Show what’s wrong — make them feel it. Numbers are good, but emotions connect.
Then — the Solution! Your hero moment.
Next is Impact! What difference does it make?
And Lasly! Now, end it with a bang. A perfect Closing.
Make it Simple. Human. Memorable. That’s the essence of a great pitch.
Timer: 02:12:35 remaining
Your team joins a call.
We’ll screen record the demo while someone narrates
Make sure the app doesn’t crash during recording.
I’ll share the final UI walkthrough.
Aira projecting guidelines: Pitch Video Structure (5 Minutes), Hook (30 sec), The Problem, The Solution Demo, Technical Stack, Impact & Future Plans.

tasks.task: What’s MOST important when starting your hackathon pitch video?
options: Clear explanation of the problem + solution
correct_answer: Clear explanation of the problem + solution

tasks.task: What should your demo focus on MOST?
During your video, what matters most to judges?
What matters most for your audio during recording?
options: Voice clearly audible and no distracting noise
correct_answer: Voice clearly audible and no distracting noise

tasks.task: What is the MOST important storytelling element in a pitch video?
options: Simple explanation of problem → solution → impact
correct_answer: Simple explanation of problem → solution → impact

dialogs.text: You hit record. You introduce the problem. Demo the core feature. Show the SDG alignment. Explain the tech stack: Frontend framework, Backend server, Database, APIs used.

minigame.description: Choose the best line for each part of your pitch.

options: Hi, my name is [Name]. I’m here to present my app.
Have you ever felt stuck studying alone, wishing someone could keep you going?
correct_answer: Have you ever felt stuck studying alone, wishing someone could keep you going?

options: Many students lose motivation when learning online due to lack of connection.
correct_answer: Many students lose motivation when learning online due to lack of connection.

options: We made an app that sends random alerts.
We built FocusFriend — an AI buddy that gamifies your study habits.
correct_answer: We built FocusFriend — an AI buddy that gamifies your study habits.

options: FocusFriend helps students feel proud of their progress and less alone.
correct_answer: FocusFriend helps students feel proud of their progress and less alone.

options: Together, let’s make learning more human — one study session at a time.
correct_answer: Together, let’s make learning more human — one study session at a time.

dialogs.text: Five minutes later—
No crashes.
Nice work, rookie! That was sharp.
Yeah. I even felt chills — and I don’t do emotions.
That was smooth! See? You always had it in you. You just needed to believe.
We actually sound… legit.
They’re proud of you. And so am I.
Thanks, Aira. For everything.

achievement.title: Pitch Master
achievement.description: Completed the Pitch Perfect mini-game and delivered a successful pitch.

dialogs.text: You open YouTube. Upload in progress…
File name: Byteon_FinalPitch.mp4
Set visibility to Public or Unlisted. Judges must be able to access it.
Upload complete. You copy the link. YouTube URL — Ready.
Wait. Remove API keys.
Never upload sensitive credentials.
Checklist appears: Clean README.md, Installation Instructions, Tech Stack Listed, Screenshots Added, License Included
You push the final commit. GitHub shows: Final Commit — Submission Version
You copy the repository link. GitHub URL — Ready.
The team refreshes the deployed site.
It loads. Login works... Core feature runs... No errors.
It’s live!
You copy the deployed link. Live Website URL — Ready.
The submission page opens. Empty text boxes stare at you
This is where you tell your story clearly.
Fields appear: What inspired you?, What it does, How we built it, Challenges we ran into, Accomplishments, What we learned, What’s next
You write honestly. About the bug that almost broke everything, the workshop that helped fix authentication, dividing roles, learning to trust each other.
You upload: Screenshots, Demo video link, GitHub link, Website link.
Everything is attached.
Double-check everything.
Video link works, GitHub public, Website accessible, SDG clearly stated, Team members listed, Submission description complete.
Timer: 00:18:52 remaining. Your cursor hovers over: Submit Project
You click on submit project.
Loading…
Loading…
Then—
Submission Successfully Received.
The countdown disappears. Confetti fills your screen.
Your Discord explodes: “WE DID IT!” “Team Byteon submitted!” “Let’s gooooo!”
Your team explodes in cheers. You lean back in your chair. Exhausted. Proud.
We actually built something
We built it. We recorded it. We shipped it.
You didn’t just participate. You published.
Today you learned something many people never do — how to turn an idea into something real.
Your laptop shows: YouTube video views increasing, GitHub stars starting to appear, Website analytics ticking up
Whether you win or not… you’ve already leveled up.
Your team appears beside you one last time.
From blank canvas to live product.
From bug panic to deployment.
From nervous silence to confident pitch.
Every expert was once a beginner who dared to click ‘Register.
Thanks, Aira. For everything.
You earned this moment. Every click, every idea, every doubt — it all brought you here.
Congratulations. You’ve completed your first hackathon journey.

achievement.title: First Hacker Medal
achievement.description: Complete Chapter 4 of How to Hackathon
